preview_text:
The model favors Portugal at 77%, with DR Congo offered a 4% chance of victory. The draw sits at 20%, reflecting meaningful uncertainty despite the clear favorite. Portugal's advantage is substantial but not overwhelming on a neutral ground.

The outcome distribution shows a decisive lean toward a Portugal win, yet the 20% draw probability signals the match is not a foregone conclusion. A DR Congo upset remains improbable; the match is most likely to settle either in Portugal's favor or as a stalemate rather than a surprise reversal.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes, with gaps under 3 percentage points. Polymarket prices track similarly. This convergence suggests broad agreement on the match structure, though the model rates DR Congo's upset chance slightly lower than the books do—a 4-point gap worth noting but not a material divergence.
